item.description: pKevin explains why the AI memory trade may be more than momentum.br /br /"If you think that supply is going to come online or think prices from memory are just going to collapse after this recent rise. You could still make the argument that these are fairly valued in that environment./pa href="http

item.title: 'Djinn' Stealer Targets Cloud, AI Credentials
item.description: The infostealer was delivered via CVE-2026-48558, a critical authentication bypass vulnerability in SimpleHelp, targeting credentials linking development and admin environments to wider enterprise systems.

item.title: RT by @Dinosn: New blog post is up looking at how LLMs are making local EDR rulesets, YARA rules, and behavioral detections trivial to extract. This post focuses on how simple the harness can be. Buck
item.description: pNew blog post is up looking at how LLMs are making local EDR rulesets, YARA rules, and behavioral detections trivial to extract. This post focuses on how simple the harness can be. item.title: Could AI Outgrow Consciousness?
item.description: pispanTL;DR: Conditional on believing in illusionist theories of consciousness, it seems plausible that AI systems will be conscious above some threshold of complexity but surpass a need for consciousness once they become sufficiently advanced./span/i/ppspanConsciousness for humans is confusing enou

item.title: DiScoFormer: One transformer for density and score, across distributions
[Linkpost]Frame Error
item.description: pspanToday I want to explain what I call “/spanbspanframe errors/span/bspan”: a third kind of mistake, distinct from logical fallacies and empirical errors. You can get every fact right and every inference valid, and still be wrong, because your framework for reasoning about the problem is structura

item.title: Pump.fun's PUMP Buybacks Top $400M as Token Stays Flat
item.description: The Solana launchpad's repurchases since July now exceed $400M, yet PUMP trades roughly 83% below its record and is little changed on the day

item.title: AI will make biological extinction risks worse before it makes them better
item.description: pAn argument goes: If we don't build aligned artificial superintelligence, we risk driving ourselves extinct for some other reason. We should rush to build ASI quickly, in spite of the risks—the longer we wait, the more vulnerable we are to extinction from a different cause./ppOther than ASI, the bi

item.title: WSJ Article Claiming China Has Matched Anthropic Is Obvious Nonsense
item.description: pa href="https://www.wsj.com/tech/ai/chinese-ai-anthropic-mythos-cybersecurity-574b02c2?mod=hp_trendingnow_article_pos2"The Wall Street Journal printed an outright false headline/a and heavily misleading story claiming this, which of course was uncritically amplified by the usual suspects./ppI post

item.title: Frank Ramsey on Induction: Why Validity Is the Wrong Standard
item.description: pspanFrank Ramsey's treatment of induction starts from a general account of inference. He argues that premises and conclusion alone do not fully specify an inference. It also needs the rule by which the conclusion is drawn. This holds for deductive and inductive arguments alike, though the rule diff

item.title: Mapping Europe’s AI Workforce Opportunity
item.description: A new OpenAI report maps how AI could reshape jobs across the EU, highlighting which occupations may face automation, growth, or workflow changes.
